title: The Middle-Ground Amidst Great (Cyber) Power Competition: From Pawns to Kingmakers?
description: Book chapter in The Changing Fights and Fighters of Contemporary War: From the Margins to the Middle, eds. Robin, Theussen & Carlson.

title: Interpreting India's Cyber Statecraft
description: With Joe Devanny. India's cyber policies emerge from a domestic political context that shapes its cyber diplomacy and wider approach to cyber statecraft.

title: Objectifs rationnels, enjeux bureaucratiques et arbitrages politiques : un examen du modèle français de cyberpolitique
source: Études françaises de renseignement et de cyber
description: Examination of the French cyber policy model through the lens of rational objectives, bureaucratic stakes, and political trade-offs.

title: Russia's Cyber War: What's Next and What the European Union Should Do
description: Assessment of Russia's cyber operations in the context of the Ukraine war and recommendations for European Union policy responses.

title: Mapping the Known Unknowns of Cybersecurity Education
description: With Trey Herr and Max Smeets. A review of syllabi on cyber conflict and security across higher education institutions.

title: France's Strategic Autonomy Takes to Space
description: Analysis of France's new space strategy and its implications for European strategic autonomy.
